title: Litscreen
headline: Built an AI screening tool solo in 8 weeks — 89% faster than manual.
summary: Litscreen is an AI-powered candidate screening tool I designed and built solo from scratch. Multi-model pipeline using Gemini and Claude APIs reduced screening time from 45 minutes to 5 minutes.
problem: Hiring teams were spending 45+ minutes manually screening each candidate — unscalable for high-volume pipelines.
approach: Defined the full product spec, designed the UX, and built the multi-model AI pipeline myself. Used Gemini for document parsing and Claude for reasoning and scoring. Iterated on prompt architecture to improve accuracy.
outcome: Shipped a working product in 8 weeks with 23K lines of code. Screening time dropped 89%. Proved that a PM with a strong point of view on AI can move as fast as a small eng team.

title: Zataak Se
headline: From founding designer to de facto PM — 3 apps, 1 design system, real impact.
summary: Joined as a founding designer at a payments + quick commerce startup. Grew into a PM role owning product across consumer, merchant, and operations apps.
problem: Three separate product surfaces (consumer app, merchant app, ops app) with no shared design language and a manual payment reconciliation process costing hours per day.
approach: Built a unified design system used across all 3 apps. Wrote PRDs for the payments flow and geo-fencing features. Partnered with engineers on Kafka integration and Maps API implementation.
outcome: 20% engagement increase in 3 months. 80% reduction in manual payment reconciliation via AI automation. 30% fewer UI inconsistencies across the product suite.

title: ShopX
headline: Took offline retailers online — D2C growth and CRO at scale.
summary: Growth Marketing Manager at ShopX, leading D2C marketing strategy, offline-to-online retailer onboarding, and conversion rate optimization campaigns.
problem: Offline retailers needed a path to digital commerce but lacked the tools, playbooks, and onboarding support to make the transition effectively.
approach: Built retailer onboarding programs to move offline sellers onto the platform. Ran CRO-driven campaigns to improve conversion across the funnel. Led D2C marketing strategy across product lifecycle stages.
outcome: Onboarded a significant base of offline retailers to digital commerce. Drove measurable conversion improvements through CRO campaigns. Strengthened D2C growth playbook adopted across the business.

title: Job Search Automation
headline: Built an end-to-end AI outreach pipeline — scrape, enrich, draft, send, track.
summary: Designed and built a fully automated job outreach system from scratch: multi-portal scraping via Apify, contact resolution via Apollo, AI-generated personalized emails, LaTeX resume tailoring per JD, OOO detection, scheduled sending, and A/B template testing — all tracked in Google Sheets.
problem: Manual job outreach is slow, inconsistent, and impossible to iterate on at scale. Most job seekers send generic emails with no personalization or tracking.
approach: Applied product thinking to my own job search. Designed the full pipeline spec, then built it: Apify actors for multi-portal scraping, Apollo for contact resolution with confidence scoring, OpenAI for email generation with playbook enforcement, LaTeX + tectonic for per-JD resume tailoring, Gmail API for draft creation, and Google Sheets as the tracking layer. A/B tests template variants per send.
outcome: Fully automated daily outreach pipeline running in production. Each email is personalized, resume-tailored, and sent at an optimized time. The system itself is a live PM proof point — 0-to-1 build, data-driven, self-applied product thinking.

title: Vordo AI
headline: AI SaaS product — end-to-end product strategy and design.
summary: Defined product requirements, designed the full UX, and drove go-to-market strategy for Vordo AI, an AI-powered SaaS tool.
problem: Teams building AI-powered workflows lacked a product-grade interface that could surface model outputs clearly and integrate with existing tools without heavy engineering lift.
approach: Owned product strategy end-to-end: user research, PRD writing, UX design, and GTM planning. Focused on reducing time-to-value for non-technical users interacting with AI outputs.
outcome: Shipped a production-ready product with a clear positioning and GTM plan. Established the design system and product principles for future iteration.
